rulings.Case Title: HARRY ELLIOTT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 29, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant IMO Industries Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Assuming, without deciding, that defendant shifted the burden of production on the issue of whether Mr. Elliott was exposed to products attributable to defendant, Mr. Elliott¿s declaration and deposition testimony create triable issues whether he was exposed to original gaskets and packing in defendant¿s pumps and turbines while Mr. Elliott worked for PG&E and whether the replacement packing and gaskets he was exposed to were supplied by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant failed to shift the burden of production on the question of whether any of its products contained asbestos because plaintiff was not asked about asbestos content at his deposition and defendant¿s interrogatories do not seek ¿all facts.¿ Defendant failed to shift the burden of production on the issue of false representation because Plaintiff was never asked about representations made by defendant at either his deposition or by interrogatory. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK RICCO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To: (1) Lift December 9, 2009, Order To Stay Or Dismiss Matter On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens; And (2) Reinstate This California Matter To The Civil Active Docket
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 29, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiff Frank Ricco¿s motion to lift the stay is denied. Assuming without deciding that plaintiff was not required to make any further efforts to establish jurisdiction in Nevada over University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Douglass Insulation Company and Advance Mechanical Contractors, a review of the papers filed on this motion shows that these defendants could be sued in federal court as part of plaintiff¿s federal court action. Because of plaintiff¿s decision to sue in both federal court and state court, there will be two separate lawsuits by plaintiff pending in two different courts regardless of the decision on this motion: one in federal court and one in Nevada state court or California state court. Given that there is much greater connection between this action and Nevada, forum nonconveniens principles strongly support that the state court action remain in Nevada. Because it appears that University, Douglass and Advance can be sued in the federal court action without destroying diversity jurisdiction over them, this court will set a hearing on its own motion on August 3, 2010 at 9:30am in department 220 why this action should not be dismissed due to the suitability of the two alternate forums of the federal court and the Nevada state court. =(220/HEK)
